data.title: Euroseas orders construction of two 4,300 teu containerships
data.description:
Euroseas (NASDAQ:ESEA) -2.5% in Monday's trading after saying it signed a contract for the construction of two 4,300 teu newbuild container vessels from China's Jiangsu New Yangzi Shipbuilding.

The company said the vessels, priced at ~$59.25M each, are due for delivery in March and May 2028., with financing to be arranged through a mix of debt and equity.

The vessels are sisterships to two 4,300 teu units Euroseas (NASDAQ:ESEA) booked at the same shipyard in October 2024, with delivery in 2027.

After the latest orders and the sale of M/V Marcos V, Euroseas; (ESEA) fleet will consist of 25 vessels with a total carrying capacity of 78,344 teu.

data.title: FactSet could become acquisition target, especially for PE - analyst
data.description:
FactSet Research (NYSE:FDS) could become an acquisition candidate following its weakness, and private equity shops would be the most logical buyers.

"We believe FactSet's recent, share multiple de-rating increases the likelihood that it could become an acquisition target, private equity would be the most logical buyer, and that a range of scenarios would yield an acceptable IRR," Raymond James analyst Patrick O'Shaughnessy, who has a market perform rating on FDS, wrote in a note on Monday.

FactSet's (NYSE:FDS) valuation has fallen to the lowest level in more than five years, hurt by "mediocre" sales momentum, commentary from a competitor that FactSet (FDS) is competing heavily on price, concerns about competition from AI and a "somewhat" unexpected CEO transition.

FactSet (FDS) is appealing to potential buyers due to its recurring revenue business model, "very modest" financial leverage, and strong long-term track record, according to O'Shaughnessy. 

FactSet (FDS) has a market cap of  $14.4 billion. 

Shares of FactSet (FDS) edged higher by 0.5% on Monday.

data.title: Rivian's R2 model is expected to be a player in the midsize electric SUV segment
data.description:
Analysts are starting to factor in the upcoming R2 release into their financial models for Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN).

The R2 is a midsize electric SUV positioned beneath the brand’s premium R1 series. The R2 is now scheduled to enter production in the first half of 2026 at the company's Normal, Illinois, facility, with deliveries expected initially that year. The company has recently completed expanding the Illinois plant and has begun assembling validation builds, aiming for production volume of around 50,000 vehicles in 2026. Due to high demand and modest starting capacity, the R2 is expected to be in short supply well into 2027, with larger-scale output tied to a forthcoming Georgia factory that may not contribute volume until 2028.

Needham reiterated its bullish view on Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) ahead of the electric vehicle maker's R2 launch, supported by what it sees as end-market diligence and a consumer survey conducted in low EV penetration metros. 

"The size of the potential market opportunity for the R2 is encouraging when looking across current EV and ICE offerings at similar price points, and even more so vs. modest consensus FY26 R2 delivery estimates," updated analyst Chris Pierce. Needham's survey work found strong Rivian (RIVN) brand awareness, limited negative perception, and encouraging purchase intent, which are factors seen as positioning Rivian (RIVN) favorably to capture market share as the R2 enters the midsize SUV segment. 

Needham reiterated its Buy rating and $14 price target on Rivian (RIVN).

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) rose 1.1% in Monday morning trading.

data.title: Covid vaccine critic tapped to lead CDC advisory committee on shots
data.description:
A task force formed within the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to discuss Covid-19 vaccines will be led by an MIT professor who has made derogatory remarks about the shots in the past.

While Retsef Levi was chosen in July to serve on the ACIP, he was recently elevated to lead the Covid task force within it, The New York Times reported.

At the top of his page on X, Levi has pinned a video of him warning of the dangers of mRNA vaccines. "The evidence is mounting and indisputable that MRNA vaccines cause serious harm including death, especially among young people," text along with the video reads. "We have to stop giving them immediately!" 

Covid vaccines from Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) and Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/BioNTech (BNTX) are mRNA-based.

"While individual members may hold initial personal views, the task force’s work will be guided by data, transparency, open-mindedness and open deliberation — not by any single opinion," HHS spokesman Andrew Nixon told the Times. "We are confident this process will produce policy recommendations that put patients’ health and safety first."

An ACIP page states that the Covid working group will discuss topics including the "risk-benefit and cost-benefit analyses of existing and newly FDA-authorized mRNA and other COVID-19 immunizations" and "identify critical gaps in the existing scientific and clinical knowledge and methodologies related to the safety and efficacy of the COVID-19 immunization."

data.title: Wall Street is mixed as Nvidia's earnings come into focus
data.description:
Wall Street trade mixed on Monday, easing back after a strong rally that drove the Dow to a record close on Friday in the wake of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) leads all of the major averages lower as it has slipped 0.4%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is down 0.1% and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) trades higher by 0.1%. 

Across the S&P 500, eight of 11 sectors are trading lower, led by Consumer Staples and Utilities, while Communication Services  has been the brightest spot so far.

In the bond market yields are inching higher with the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rising by 3 basis points to 4.28%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) has moved up 3 basis points to 3.73%. 

On the economic front, the Chicago Fed National Activity Index ticked down in July as production slipped into the red. Also, U.S. new home sales slid 0.5% to 652K in the month of July. Eyes now turn to the July PCE price index report which is scheduled to come out on Friday. 

This all follows Fridays speech by Fed Chair Powell which led to a strong market rally. 

"Powell’s speech at Jackson Hole last week was classic Powell. That is not a terribly good thing. Powell signaled an increased probability of a September rate cut to counter the damage of trade taxes on the U.S. economy," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Markets liked that. But this was essentially the “data dependent” mantra padded with additional rhetoric, Donovan added. 

Furthermore, investors will also keep their eye on the Nvidia (NVDA) results slated to come on Wednesday after the market close. 

"Finally, the big event in corporate earnings will be Nvidia's results, which come as tech stocks had seen their biggest five-day pullback since April prior to Friday’s rally," Sidorov added.

data.title: Texas manufacturing index
data.description:
The Texas Manufacturing Index remained near zero, at -1.8 in August, compared with 0.9 in July, the Dallas Fed said on Monday.

Factory activity in Texas continued to expand, with new orders improving for the first time since July, and the shipments index reaching its highest level in more than three years.

Production index: 15.3 vs. 21.3 prior.

Shipments: 14.2 vs. 2.7.

Capacity utilization: 13.7 vs. 17.3

New orders: 5.8 vs. -3.6.

Outlook index: 3.3 vs. 4.7

Future production index: 40.4 vs. 30.3 prior, the Dallas Fed said.

data.title: Marvell set to release positive guidance in latest earnings: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) will likely present better-than-expected guidance when it releases its latest quarterly results following the recent divestiture of its Automotive Ethernet business and concern around Amazon's (AMZN) Trainium chip, according to Morgan Stanley.

Marvell is slated to release its second quarter fiscal 2026 financial results post-market on Thursday, August 28. A consensus estimate calls for adjusted earnings per share of $0.67 on revenue of $2.01B.

Earlier this month, Marvell completed the sale of its Automotive Ethernet business to Infineon (OTCQX:IFNNY)(OTCQX:IFNNF) for $2.5B. The business was expected to contribute $225M to $250MM in revenue during fiscal 2026.

"Expect upside this quarter from optical; lowering estimates slightly post the automotive divestiture, but expect positive guidance excluding that impact," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, in a Monday investor note.

"AI revenue is estimated to be $876mn (+6.6% q/q) in JulQ and $955mn (+9.0% q/q) in OctQ, with faster growth coming from ASICs," Moore added. "We see potential upside to our optical estimates from the strong AI ramp, which we view more favorably than their ASIC prospects. Outside of short-term supply issues, we believe that optical is stronger than generally perceived, and is more durable and higher margin than their ASIC business ...  The Trainium 3 debate may continue to be noisy, but we believe we are past the phase of inflated expectations and anticipate steady sequential ASIC revenue reaching $2bn for the year."

In other AI stocks, Morgan Stanley believes Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) faces negative sentiment in the quarters ahead.

"For Micron, high bandwidth memory pricing for the current HBM 3e will have a hard reset next year with at least one customer, NVIDIA (NVDA), which had committed to full year 2025 pricing," Moore said. "But sentiment is quite negative, and we expect HBM to retain a meaningful, albeit narrowing, premium to DDR5."

data.title: Equinor nixes $10B floating wind project offshore Australia
data.description:
Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) -1.1% in early trading Monday after saying it will not proceed with the $10B Novocastrian floating wind farm project offshore Australia's New South Wales.

The project, which was supposed to deploy floating wind turbines across a 500 sq km area, previously received a feasibility license from Australian authorities for the 2 GW project.

The move follows Equinor's (NYSE:EQNR) recent withdrawal from another Australian project, the Bass offshore wind energy project to be located near Tasmania in the Bass Strait.

Equinor (EQNR) owns a stake 10% stake in Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY), which was told Friday by the Trump administration to stop all activities related to the Revolution Wind project off the coast of Rhode Island, which is under construction and was expected to become operational next year.

Equinor's (EQNR) Empire Wind farm experienced a similar halt earlier this year, but was later re-approved after a series of high level meetings at the White House and a last minute deal struck with New York Governor Hochul.

"If Equinor participates in Ørsted's rights issue with its proportional share, the company will inevitably increase its exposure to the U.S. market - something management has previously stated they want to limit," Sparebank1 Markets analyst Teodor Sveen-Nilsen said in a note.

Separately, Equinor (EQNR) and its partners said Monday they struck oil and gas in the Fram area near the Troll field in the North Sea.

data.title: Stock exchanges write to regulators urging clampdown on tokenized equities - report
data.description:
UK-based World Federation of Exchanges has written to three regulatory bodies, urging a clampdown on the so-called 'tokenized stocks', Reuters reported on Monday, citing the letter.

The letter is said to have been sent to the U.S SEC's Crypto Task Force, the European Securities and Markets Authority, and IOSCO's Fintech Task Force.

Tokenised equities are blockchain-based tokens created to represent shares in companies, but investors do not become shareholders in the underlying company, the newswire noted.

In June, Coinbase Global (COIN) was reportedly seeking permission to offer "tokenized equities" to its customers. The move would allow its customers to trade stocks on the blockchain.

Similarly, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) unveiled U.S. stock and ETF tokens in the European Union, enabling eligible users to trade more than 200 digital versions of real company shares and ETFs on the blockchain.

Later, OpenAI issued a statement denying any affiliation with the tokenized equity offered by Robinhood, emphasizing that the tokens do not represent real ownership or equity in the company.

"We are alarmed at the plethora of brokers and crypto-trading platforms offering or intending to offer so-called tokenised U.S. stocks," the WFE reportedly wrote in the letter.

"These products are marketed as stock tokens or the equivalent to stocks when they are not," said the group representing the biggest stock exchanges.

The WFE noted that the companies whose stock is being mimicked could suffer reputational damage if the tokens fail, and regulators should apply securities rules to tokenised assets, according to Reuters.

data.title: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals settles lawsuit with Lupin
data.description:
Catalyst Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CPRX) said that the company and licensor SERB S.A. signed a settlement agreement with Lupin Pharmaceuticals, putting an end to a patent lawsuit.

The lawsuit was in response to Lupin's request to the FDA for permission to sell a generic version of Firdapse (amifampridine) 10 mg tablets before the existing patents expire.

According to the terms of the settlement deal, Lupin will not be able to sell its generic version of Firdapse in the U.S., until at least February 25, 2035, assuming it receives approval from the U.S. FDA.

Additionally, the agreement states that all current patent lawsuits between Catalyst/SERB and Lupin concerning Firdapse patents in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey will be dismissed.

Shares of CPRX are up 2.75%.
